info.name: 03 Tickets
info.description:
Module owner: Imbnethminichinth@gmail.com

Scope: Incident tickets, comments, technician-facing updates, and comment ownership rules.

HTTP methods scaffolded: GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE.

body.raw:
{
  "resourceId": "{{resourceId}}",
  "location": "Main Block - Lab 3",
  "category": "ELECTRICAL",
  "priority": "HIGH",
  "description": "Projector is not powering on and the indicator is blinking red.",
  "preferredContact": "{{userEmail}}",
  "attachmentRefs": [
    "evidence-projector-front.jpg",
    "error-screen.jpg"
  ]
}

request.description:
Intended endpoint: `POST /tickets`
Expected role: authenticated user
Expected status: `201 Created`
TODO: Replace the placeholder attachment representation with the final safe file-upload design.

Intended endpoint: `GET /tickets?mine=true`
Expected role: authenticated user
Expected status: `200 OK`
TODO: Switch this to the final user ticket listing route if the query shape changes.

Intended endpoint: `PATCH /tickets/{{ticketId}}/status`
Expected role: `ADMIN` or `TECHNICIAN`
Expected status: `200 OK`
TODO: Bind this to the final status-transition route and enum values.

Intended endpoint: `DELETE /tickets/{{ticketId}}/comments/{{commentId}}`
Expected role: comment owner or privileged staff
Expected status: `204 No Content`
TODO: Keep this request aligned with the final comment ownership rules.

Intended endpoint: `POST /tickets`
Expected role: authenticated user
Expected status: `400 Bad Request`
TODO: Replace this with the real invalid payload once the validation annotations are finalized.

Intended endpoint: `DELETE /tickets/{{ticketId}}/comments/{{commentId}}`
Expected role: comment owner or privileged staff
Expected status: `403 Forbidden` when deleting another user's comment with `{{accessToken}}`
TODO: Use this request to prove comment ownership enforcement.

Intended endpoint: `POST /tickets/{{ticketId}}/comments`
Expected role: authenticated user or staff participant
Expected status: `201 Created`
TODO: Move this request into the happy-path folder once the comment API is stable.
